mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 7.5%, consumer spending power collapses and corporate earnings forecasts are downgraded, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 7.5% for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal income and housing starts signals synchronized consumer and capital expenditure growth, generating positive equity returns as corporate revenue pipelines strengthen.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in greater than 60% of triggered cases despite concurrent growth in both series.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates tightening capacity utilization and pricing power for manufacturers, which translates to immediate positive S&P 500 momentum during early-cycle recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns when capacity utilization is already above 85%, or signal accuracy drops below chance in late-cycle regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth reflects robust aggregate demand and corporate profit expansion, systematically driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth shows zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward returns across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid monthly inflation spikes trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative forward returns.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL monthly increases above 0.4% consistently precede positive 20-day returns without subsequent rate hike expectations materializing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total employment combined with falling unemployment rates confirms a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer confidence and corporate earnings, driving positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The divergent labor signal fails to predict positive returns when wage growth stagnates, or produces negative returns in greater than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production alongside rising GDP indicates supply chain bottlenecks that constrain corporate output and profit margins, generating negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The supply-demand divergence signal yields positive forward returns in greater than 55% of triggered instances despite confirmed output constraints.
